lesson.menu_desc: Ordering with would like and I'll have -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 -- Eating Out & Ordering -- Making Orders (would like / I'll have)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 13 -- Eating Out & Ordering

listenings.text: Good evening, and welcome to our restaurant. My name is Sarah, and I will be your server tonight. Let me tell you about today's specials. To start, we have a fresh tomato soup and a green salad as appetizers. For the main course, the chef recommends the grilled salmon with vegetables, or the roast chicken with potatoes. If you prefer something lighter, the pasta is very popular. For dessert, we have a chocolate cake and a fruit tart. Can I get you something to drink while you look at the menu? And please, if you have any allergies, just let me know, and I will check with the kitchen.

Thank you. Everything sounds wonderful. To start, I would like the tomato soup, please. For the main course, I will have the grilled salmon. Could I get the vegetables instead of the potatoes? I am trying to eat a little lighter. I should mention I have a mild allergy to nuts, so please make sure there are no nuts in my dish. For dessert, I think I will have the fruit tart. And could I have a glass of sparkling water to drink? That is everything for now. Thank you so much. Oh, and could we have the bill whenever you are ready?
